perturbation.original_text: ii. Contractors of Data Recipient who (1) have signed a non-disclosure agreement that covers the Non -Public Information and is at least as restrictive as this Agreement, and (2) need the Non -Public Info rmation for the work being performed subject to the limitation on Market Sensitive Information below. In the event Data Recipient shares Non -Public Information WECC Confidentiality Agreement 2 with a contractor pursuant to this provision, Data Recipient agrees to be responsible and jointl y and severally liable for any breach of confidentiality by that contractor ;
perturbation.changed_text: ii. Contractors of Data Recipient who (1) have signed a non-disclosure agreement that covers the Non -Public Information and is at least as restrictive as this Agreement, and (2) need the Non -Public Info rmation for the work being performed subject to the limitation on Market Sensitive Information below.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'In the event Data Recipient shares Non -Public Information WECC Confidentiality Agreement 2 with a contractor pursuant to this provision, Data Recipient agrees to be responsible and jointl y and severally liable for any breach of confidentiality by that contractor ;' creates an omission that relieves the Data Recipient from being responsible for breaches of confidentiality by its contractors, directly conflicting with the general intent of the agreement to protect Non-Public Information. This creates uncertainty in enforcement if a contractor breaches the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Treatment of Non -Public Information a. Data Recipient agrees to take all necessary precautions to maintain the confidentiality of the Non -Public Information and to prevent unauthorized access to it.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Data Recipient's responsibility for confidentiality breaches by its contractors. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Data Recipient agrees to take all necessary precautions to maintain the confidentiality of the Non -Public Information and to prevent unauthorized access to it. By removing the liability clause, the Data Recipient is no longer explicitly responsible for the actions of its contractors, creating a conflict and weakening the overall confidentiality agreement, leading to an omission type of in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: iii. As required by FERC orders, rules or regulations, provided that additional data recipients have signed a non-disclosure agreement that covers the Non -Public Information and is at least as restrictive as this Agreement; or
perturbation.changed_text: iii. As required by FERC orders, rules or regulations; or
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'provided that additional data recipients have signed a non-disclosure agreement that covers the Non -Public Information and is at least as restrictive as this Agreement', the clause now implies that the Data Recipient can disclose Non-Public Information to additional data recipients without ensuring those recipients have signed a non-disclosure agreement. This contradicts the core intent of the entire agreement to protect confidentiality, generating an internal inconsistency.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Treatment of Non -Public Information a. Data Recipient agrees to take all necessary precautions to maintain the confidentiality of the Non -Public Information and to prevent unauthorized access to it.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted data disclosure by requiring non-disclosure agreements for additional recipients of Non-Public Information. The changed text removes this requirement, allowing disclosure without such agreements. This directly contradicts Section 2.a, which mandates precautions to maintain confidentiality and prevent unauthorized access to Non-Public Information. The removal of the NDA requirement creates a direct conflict and makes the agreement internally inconsistent, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: and to ensure that Data Recipient or such an employee of Data Recipient discontinue s accessing or using any Market Sensitive Informa tion as identified in the WECC Information Sharing Policy .
perturbation.changed_text: and to ensure that Data Recipient or such an employee of Data Recipient discontinue s accessing or using any Market Sensitive Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the reference to the 'WECC Information Sharing Policy' in Section 6.b creates ambiguity regarding what constitutes Market Sensitive Information, as the policy isn't mentioned anywhere. This introduces an internal inconsistency because the Data Recipient is now instructed to discontinue accessing 'Market Sensitive Information' without clear guidance on what that encompasses, creating uncertainty and undermining the purpose of the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Market Sensitive Information a. Data Recipient agrees not to provide or disclose any Market Sensitive Information as identified in the WECC Information Sharing Policy to any person who is ( 1) a Market Function Employee as defined by the FERC Standards of Conduct, or ( 2) actively and personal ly engaged in day-to - day sales of electric power or other electric power marketing functions.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6.b referenced the 'WECC Information Sharing Policy' to define 'Market Sensitive Information'. The changed text removes this reference, creating ambiguity. This directly contradicts Section 3.a, which explicitly relies on the 'WECC Information Sharing Policy' to define 'Market Sensitive Information'. The removal of the policy reference in Section 6.b makes the definition in Section 3.a unclear and creates a strong inconsistency, as the Data Recipient is now instructed to discontinue accessing 'Market Sensitive Information' without clear guidance. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: b. Upon any termination of this Agreement, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writing that all Non -Public Information has been returned or destroyed , except as may be otherwise required by law in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement shall survive termination .
perturbation.changed_text: b. Upon any termination of this Agreement, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writing that all Non -Public Information has been returned or destroyed .
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except as may be otherwise required by law in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement shall survive termination', the agreement implies that the confidentiality obligations terminate completely upon termination, even if the law requires otherwise. This creates a contradiction because Data Recipient may be legally obliged to maintain confidentiality, and removing this text suggest Data Recipient is no longer bounded to the terms of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Treatment of Non -Public Information a. Data Recipient agrees to take all necessary precautions to maintain the confidentiality of the Non -Public Information and to prevent unauthorized access to it.
perturbation.justification: The original text acknowledges legal exceptions to data return/destruction, preserving confidentiality obligations even after termination when required by law. The changed text removes this exception, implying complete termination of confidentiality obligations regardless of legal requirements. This directly contradicts the 'Treatment of Non-Public Information' section, which mandates ongoing confidentiality precautions. Therefore, the omission creates a strong in-text contradiction because Data Recipient may be legally obliged to maintain confidentiality, and removing this text suggest Data Recipient is no longer bounded to the terms of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of W ECC and any owner or submitter of the Non - Public Information. This Agreement shall be fully enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non -Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: c. This Agreement shall be for the sole benefit of W ECC and any owner or submitter of the Non - Public Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'This Agreement shall be fully enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non -Public Information is not treated in accordance with this Agreement.' implies that the agreement might not be enforceable, creating an in-text contradiction. The agreement outlines the Data Recipient's obligations to protect Non-Public Information, but by eliminating the enforceability clause, it introduces ambiguity about the agreement's legal standing.
perturbation.contradicted_location: IN WITNESS HEREOF, Data Recipient has read and understands this Agreement and enters into this Agreement voluntarily, wishing to be legally bound.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IN WITNESS HEREOF, Data Recipient has read and understands this Agreement and enters into this Agreement voluntarily, wishing to be legally bound.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the agreement is fully enforceable by WECC and any entity whose Non-Public Information is not treated in accordance with the agreement. The changed text removes this enforceability clause. The contradicted text states that the Data Recipient enters the agreement voluntarily, wishing to be legally bound. Removing the enforceability clause creates a contradiction because it introduces ambiguity about whether the Data Recipient will actually be legally bound, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
